name: vgg

members.name: VGGConfig
members.signature: VGGConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, arch: tuple[int, ...] = (2, 2, 3, 3, 3), batch_norm: bool = False, dropout: float = 0.5)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, arch: tuple[int, ...] = (2, 2, 3, 3, 3), batch_norm: bool = False, dropout: float = 0.5)

members.extended:
`arch` encodes the per-block conv counts:
  - `(1, 1, 2, 2, 2)` → VGG-11
  - `(2, 2, 2, 2, 2)` → VGG-13
  - `(2, 2, 3, 3, 3)` → VGG-16
  - `(2, 2, 4, 4, 4)` → VGG-19

`batch_norm` enables BatchNorm after each Conv+ReLU pair (VGG-BN).
`dropout` applies to the two 4096-dim FC layers (0.5 in the paper).

members.name: VGG

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Run Fast R-CNN on a batch of images.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: (B, C, H, W) image batch.

parameters.name: proposals
parameters.description: list of B tensors, each (N_i, 4) xyxy proposals.

parameters.name: targets
parameters.description:
Optional training targets (list of dicts with
       "boxes" and "labels").

returns.description:
`ObjectDetectionOutput`:
`logits`     : (Σ N_i, num_classes + 1) raw class logits.
`pred_boxes` : (Σ N_i, 4) decoded xyxy top-class boxes.
`loss`       : scalar multi-task loss (only with targets).

members.extended:
Implements the unified VGG topology from Simonyan & Zisserman,
"Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image
Recognition", ICLR 2015.  All variants (VGG-11/13/16/19, with or
without `lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d`) share the same macro
structure: five blocks of stacked $3\times3$ convolutions
interleaved with $2\times2$ max-pools, with channel widths
fixed at `(64, 128, 256, 512, 512)`.  Only the number of
convolutions per block changes between variants.  A final
`lucid.nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d` collapses the feature map to
$7\times7$ so the backbone output is fixed-size regardless of
input resolution.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the factory functions
(`vgg_11`, `vgg_13`, `vgg_16`, `vgg_19`
and their `*_bn` variants) for paper-cited configurations.

examples:
Build a VGG-16 backbone and run a single forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_16
>>> backbone = vgg_16()
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = backbone(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape   # (B, 512, 7, 7)
(2, 512, 7, 7)
notes:
From Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015, Table 1.  The motivating
insight is that two stacked $3\times3$ convolutions cover the
same receptive field as a single $5\times5$ convolution but
use only $2 \cdot 9 C^2 = 18 C^2$ parameters versus
$25 C^2$, while inserting an extra nonlinearity between them.
Three stacked $3\times3$ layers match a $7\times7$
receptive field at $27 C^2$ versus $49 C^2$ parameters
and *two* extra nonlinearities.  Depth at a fixed receptive field
therefore buys representational power essentially for free.  VGG
backbones became the standard feature extractor for downstream
tasks (Faster R-CNN, neural style transfer) for several years
after publication.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: features
attributes.description:
The five conv blocks; structure depends on `config.arch` and
`config.batch_norm` — see `_build_features`.

attributes.name: avgpool
attributes.description:
Global pool down to $7\times7$ so the backbone produces a
fixed-size feature regardless of input resolution.

attributes.name: feature_info
attributes.description:
Per-stage descriptor (channels + reduction factor) exposed via
`BackboneMixin` for downstream decoder modules.

members.name: VGGForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: VGG with two 4096-dim fully-connected layers and a linear classifier.
members.extended:
Combines a `VGG` convolutional backbone with the standard
paper-cited classifier head: FC6 (512·7·7 = 25088 → 4096), FC7
(4096 → 4096), and a final linear projection to
`config.num_classes` (default 1000 for ImageNet-1k).
`lucid.nn.Dropout` with `config.dropout` (0.5 in the
paper) is applied after both ReLU activations.  When `labels` are
supplied to `forward`, cross-entropy loss is returned
alongside the logits.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture spec.  Use the `*_cls` factory functions
(`vgg_11_cls`, `vgg_16_cls`, …) for paper-cited
configurations.

examples:
Run inference on a 224x224 RGB batch:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_16_cls
>>> model = vgg_16_cls()
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(2, 1000)
notes:
From Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015.  The two 4096-dim FC layers
dominate the parameter count: VGG-16 has 138 M parameters total, of
which roughly 124 M sit in FC6 + FC7.  This is a recognised
weakness — almost all later architectures (ResNet, DenseNet, …)
replace the giant FC head with a single `lucid.nn.Linear`
after `lucid.nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d` to slash the parameter
cost.  VGG-16 reaches a top-5 ImageNet validation error of 7.3% and
VGG-19 reaches 7.5%.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: features, avgpool
attributes.description: Same backbone components as on `VGG`.

attributes.name: fc6, fc7
attributes.description: The two 4096-dim hidden fully-connected layers.

attributes.name: drop6, drop7
attributes.description:
Dropout layers applied after each ReLU in the hidden FC stack,
controlled by `config.dropout`.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description: Final linear projection 4096 → `num_classes`.

members.name: vgg_11
members.summary: VGG-11 feature-extracting backbone (config A, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the paper-cited VGG-11 topology:
per-block convolution counts `(1, 1, 2, 2, 2)` across the five
blocks of widths `(64, 128, 256, 512, 512)`.  Approximately
132.9 M parameters in the full classifier variant (≈9.2 M in the
convolutional trunk alone).  Reaches a top-5 ImageNet validation
error of 9.4% in Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015 (Table 3,
configuration A).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored — the returned model is randomly initialised.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig` to
customise individual fields (`in_channels`, `num_classes`,
`dropout`).

returns.description:
Backbone with the VGG-11 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_11
>>> model = vgg_11()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape   # (B, 512, 7, 7)
(1, 512, 7, 7)
notes:
See Simonyan & Zisserman, "Very Deep Convolutional Networks for
Large-Scale Image Recognition", ICLR 2015, Table 1 (configuration A).

members.name: vgg_11_bn
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the VGG-11 topology
`arch=(1, 1, 2, 2, 2)` and a `lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d` layer
after each Conv + ReLU pair.  BatchNorm was added in the
timm / reference-framework reimplementations of VGG (not in the
original paper) and substantially improves convergence speed and
final accuracy; the topology is otherwise identical to
`vgg_11`.  Approximately 132.9 M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the VGG-11-BN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_11_bn
>>> model = vgg_11_bn()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 512, 7, 7)

members.name: vgg_11_bn_cls
members.signature: vgg_11_bn_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: VGG11BNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Same topology as `vgg_11_cls` but with
`lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d` after each Conv + ReLU pair.
BatchNorm was added in the timm / reference-framework
reimplementations of VGG (not in the original paper) and
substantially improves convergence speed and final accuracy.
Approximately 132.9 M parameters; top-1 ImageNet validation
accuracy of 70.37% (torchvision eval).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG11BNWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Classifier with the VGG-11-BN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it), optionally initialised from
pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_11_bn_cls
>>> model = vgg_11_bn_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from torchvision's
`VGG11_BN_Weights` and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under
`lucid-dl/vgg-11-bn`.

members.name: vgg_11_cls
members.signature: vgg_11_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: VGG11Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: VGG-11 image classifier (config A, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGGForImageClassification` with the paper-cited
VGG-11 topology (`arch=(1, 1, 2, 2, 2)`) followed by the two
4096-dim fully-connected layers and a final linear projection to
`config.num_classes`.  Approximately 132.9 M parameters total
(≈124 M of which sit in the two large FC layers).  Reaches a top-1
ImageNet validation accuracy of 69.02% (torchvision eval).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG11Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig` — use
`num_classes=N` to retarget the head.

returns.description:
Classifier with the VGG-11 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it), optionally initialised from
pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_11_cls
>>> model = vgg_11_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from torchvision's `VGG11_Weights`
and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under `lucid-dl/vgg-11`.

members.name: vgg_13
members.summary: VGG-13 feature-extracting backbone (config B, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the paper-cited VGG-13 topology:
per-block convolution counts `(2, 2, 2, 2, 2)`.  Approximately
133.0 M parameters in the full classifier variant.  Reaches a top-5
ImageNet validation error of 8.8% (Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015,
Table 3, configuration B).

parameters.description:

ignored.

>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape   # (B, 512, 7, 7)
(1, 512, 7, 7)
notes: See Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015, Table 1 (configuration B).

members.summary: VGG-13 image classifier (config B, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGGForImageClassification` with the paper-cited
VGG-13 topology (`arch=(2, 2, 2, 2, 2)`) followed by the standard
4096 → 4096 → `num_classes` FC head.  Approximately 133.0 M
parameters total.  Reaches a top-1 ImageNet validation accuracy of
69.93% (torchvision eval).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG13Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

Classifier with the VGG-13 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it), optionally initialised from

>>> out.logits.shape

members.name: vgg_16
members.summary: VGG-16 feature-extracting backbone (config D, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the paper-cited VGG-16 topology:
per-block convolution counts `(2, 2, 3, 3, 3)` — adding a third
$3\times3$ convolution to each of the deeper blocks 3/4/5.
Approximately 138.4 M parameters in the full classifier variant
(≈14.7 M in the convolutional trunk alone).  Reaches a top-5
ImageNet validation error of 7.3% (Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015,
Table 3, configuration D) — VGG-16 was the most widely deployed
variant of the family.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the VGG-16 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_16
>>> model = vgg_16()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape   # (B, 512, 7, 7)
(1, 512, 7, 7)
notes: See Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015, Table 1 (configuration D).

members.name: vgg_16_bn
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the VGG-16 topology
`arch=(2, 2, 3, 3, 3)` and `lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d` after
each Conv + ReLU pair.  Approximately 138.4 M parameters.  Often
preferred over plain `vgg_16` for downstream fine-tuning
because BatchNorm stabilises gradient statistics in the early conv
stack.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the VGG-16-BN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_16_bn
>>> model = vgg_16_bn()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 512, 7, 7)

members.name: vgg_16_bn_cls
members.signature: vgg_16_bn_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: VGG16BNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Same topology as `vgg_16_cls` but with
`lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d` after each Conv + ReLU pair.
Approximately 138.4 M parameters; top-1 ImageNet validation
accuracy of 73.36% (torchvision eval).  Often preferred over plain
`vgg_16_cls` for downstream fine-tuning.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG16BNWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

Classifier with the VGG-16-BN configuration applied (or with

examples:

>>> model = vgg_16_bn_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from torchvision's
`VGG16_BN_Weights` and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under
`lucid-dl/vgg-16-bn`.

members.name: vgg_16_cls
members.signature: vgg_16_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: VGG16Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: VGG-16 image classifier (config D, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGGForImageClassification` with the paper-cited
VGG-16 topology (`arch=(2, 2, 3, 3, 3)`) followed by the standard
4096 → 4096 → `num_classes` FC head.  Approximately 138.4 M
parameters total and a top-1 ImageNet validation accuracy of 71.59%
(torchvision eval) — the most widely used VGG variant.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG16Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Classifier with the VGG-16 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it), optionally initialised from
pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_16_cls
>>> model = vgg_16_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(2, 1000)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from torchvision's `VGG16_Weights`
and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under `lucid-dl/vgg-16`.

members.name: vgg_19
members.summary: VGG-19 feature-extracting backbone (config E, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the paper-cited VGG-19 topology:
per-block convolution counts `(2, 2, 4, 4, 4)` — four
$3\times3$ convolutions in each of the deeper blocks.
Approximately 143.7 M parameters in the full classifier variant.
Reaches a top-5 ImageNet validation error of 7.5% (Simonyan &
Zisserman, ICLR 2015, Table 3, configuration E) — slightly worse
than VGG-16 due to optimisation difficulty at this depth without
residual connections.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the VGG-19 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_19
>>> model = vgg_19()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape   # (B, 512, 7, 7)
(1, 512, 7, 7)
notes: See Simonyan & Zisserman, ICLR 2015, Table 1 (configuration E).

members.name: vgg_19_bn
members.extended:
Builds a `VGG` with the VGG-19 topology
`arch=(2, 2, 4, 4, 4)` and `lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d` after
each Conv + ReLU pair.  Approximately 143.7 M parameters.  The
deepest VGG variant; BatchNorm is particularly helpful at this
depth.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the VGG-19-BN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:

>>> model = vgg_19_bn()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 512, 7, 7)

members.name: vgg_19_bn_cls
members.signature: vgg_19_bn_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: VGG19BNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Same topology as `vgg_19_cls` but with
`lucid.nn.BatchNorm2d` after each Conv + ReLU pair.
Approximately 143.7 M parameters; top-1 ImageNet validation
accuracy of 74.22% (torchvision eval).  The deepest VGG variant —
BatchNorm is especially valuable at this depth.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG19BNWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Classifier with the VGG-19-BN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it), optionally initialised from
pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_19_bn_cls
>>> model = vgg_19_bn_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from torchvision's
`VGG19_BN_Weights` and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under
`lucid-dl/vgg-19-bn`.

members.name: vgg_19_cls
members.signature: vgg_19_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: VGG19Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: VGG-19 image classifier (config E, no BatchNorm).
members.extended:
Builds a `VGGForImageClassification` with the paper-cited
VGG-19 topology (`arch=(2, 2, 4, 4, 4)`) followed by the standard
4096 → 4096 → `num_classes` FC head.  Approximately 143.7 M
parameters total.  Reaches a top-1 ImageNet validation accuracy of
72.38% (torchvision eval).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`VGG19Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `VGGConfig`.

returns.description:
Classifier with the VGG-19 configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it), optionally initialised from
pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.vgg import vgg_19_cls
>>> model = vgg_19_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from torchvision's `VGG19_Weights`
and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under `lucid-dl/vgg-19`.

members.name: VGG11BNWeights
members.extended: VGG-A with BatchNorm (132.9 M params, 7.6 GFLOPs, top-1 70.37%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import vgg_11_bn_cls
>>> model = vgg_11_bn_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Simonyan, Zisserman, *"Very Deep Convolutional Networks
for Large-Scale Image Recognition"*, ICLR 2015 ([arXiv:1409.1556](https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.1556)).
BatchNorm added in the torchvision reimplementation (not in the
original paper).

attributes.name: DEFAULT
attributes.description: Alias for `IMAGENET1K_V1`.

citation: Simonyan, Karen, and Andrew Zisserman. "Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition." International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR), 2015.
theory:
VGG demonstrated that *uniformity and depth* — rather than
bespoke layer designs — were the dominant factors driving image-
classification accuracy in 2014.  The network is built from a
single recipe: stack $3\times3$ convolutions with stride 1
and padding 1, periodically halve the spatial resolution with a
$2\times2$ max-pool, double the channel count at each
pooling boundary, and end with three large fully-connected
layers.

The $3\times3$ kernel choice is theoretically motivated.
Two stacked $3\times3$ convolutions have the same receptive
field as a single $5\times5$ convolution but use only
$2 \cdot 9 C^2 = 18 C^2$ parameters versus $25 C^2$,
while inserting an extra nonlinearity between them.  Three stacked
$3\times3$ layers match a $7\times7$ receptive field at
$27 C^2$ versus $49 C^2$ parameters and *two* extra
nonlinearities.  Depth at a fixed receptive field therefore buys
representational power essentially for free.

The paper's variants (configurations A, B, D, E corresponding to
VGG-11/13/16/19) differ only in how many $3\times3$ convs are
stacked between pooling layers.  Despite a parameter count of
138 M — most of it locked up in the 4096-dim fully-connected layers
— VGG-16 achieves a top-5 ImageNet error of 7.3%, second only to
GoogLeNet in ILSVRC-2014, and its features transferred so well
that "VGG features" became the standard backbone for downstream
tasks (object detection, segmentation, style transfer) for years
after publication.
